mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with combined GDPC1 and INDPRO growth in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to rising discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong demand for residential construction, boosting equity valuations through improved sector performance and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns despite HOUST growth in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below 105 signals weak supply chain capacity, creating inflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through heightened risk-off sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 105.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved consumer spending prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with combined PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS cuts in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Surging consumer spending (RSAFS) above 500,000 million dollars signals strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through elevated corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where RSAFS exceeds 500,000.
